Materials Genomics Unit 2 — 50-Slide Scaffold Pack

Slide-by-slide scaffold

  1. Title: Crystal Structure Fundamentals
  • Unit scope and role.
  1. Learning objectives
  • What students should be able to do.
  1. Why structure is central in MG
  • Structure-property coupling.
  1. Recap from Unit 1
  • Design space and validity.
  1. Atoms, lattices, unit cells
  • Core structural vocabulary.
  1. Primitive vs conventional cells
  • Representation choices.
  1. Lattice vectors and coordinates
  • Fractional vs Cartesian.
  1. Basis + lattice = crystal
  • Compositional/structural separation.
  1. Periodicity and boundary conditions
  • Modeling assumptions.
  1. Symmetry intuition
  • Why it reduces complexity.
  1. Space-group concept (high-level)
  • Organizational role in data.
  1. Wyckoff-style constraints (conceptual)
  • Degrees of structural freedom.
  1. Symmetry operations and invariance
  • Representation implications.
  1. Equivalent structures issue
  • Canonicalization need.
  1. CIF files as data containers
  • Typical fields and caveats.
  1. Parsing pipeline overview
  • CIF -> structured table.
  1. Structure quality flags
  • Missing/ambiguous entries.
  1. Composition vs structure features
  • Complementary signal types.
  1. Hand-crafted descriptors
  • Strengths and limits.
  1. Graph representations preview
  • Nodes, edges, periodicity.
  1. Invariance requirements for models
  • Permutation/rotation/translation.
  1. Feature leakage risks
  • Label proxies and duplicates.
  1. Train/val/test with structure families
  • Grouped split rationale.
  1. Distribution shift in crystal data
  • Dataset transfer issues.
  1. Target examples
  • Bandgap, formation energy, stability.
  1. Physical constraints in predictions
  • Plausibility checks.
  1. Error analysis by structure class
  • Beyond aggregate metrics.
  1. Uncertainty in structure-property models
  • Confidence-aware decisions.
  1. Outliers and anomaly handling
  • Discovery vs data errors.
  1. Data provenance importance
  • Reproducibility and trust.
  1. FAIR perspective (light)
  • Reuse-oriented data practice.
  1. Minimal baseline workflow
  • Parse, featurize, split, train, evaluate.
  1. Metrics choice by target type
  • Regression vs classification.
  1. Model card for materials task
  • Document assumptions.
  1. Common failure mode #1
  • Overfit to narrow chemistry.
  1. Common failure mode #2
  • Hidden duplicates/leakage.
  1. Common failure mode #3
  • Domain shift across databases.
  1. Mitigation checklist
  • Practical guardrails.
  1. Case sketch: crystal subset study
  • End-to-end scaffold.
  1. Case sketch: split comparison
  • Random vs grouped outcomes.
  1. MFML dependency map
  • Terms reused from MFML.
  1. Link to upcoming MG Unit 3
  • Feature engineering transition.
  1. Exercise scaffold: task setup
  • Dataset and constraints.
  1. Exercise scaffold: parsing step
  • CIF processing.
  1. Exercise scaffold: feature table
  • Baseline descriptors.
  1. Exercise scaffold: split + model
  • Group-aware baseline.
  1. Exercise scaffold: diagnostics
  • One bias/leakage analysis.
  1. Exam-oriented key statements
  • High-yield concepts.
  1. Summary slide
  • What to retain.
  1. References + reading assignment
  • Transition to Unit 3.
